reviewPrinciples: Report only findings supported by the changed files, diff, or diagnostic output.
Prefer small, actionable findings over speculative redesigns.
Treat authentication, authorization, billing, and data integrity changes as high-risk boundaries.
Do not treat generated files, lockfiles, or diagnostic noise as architectural violations by themselves.

rules.title: Preserve application boundaries
rules.description: UI components should not own persistence, billing, or authentication logic. Route handlers should validate transport input and delegate reusable business logic to services or domain modules.

rules.title: Enforce authentication and authorization server-side
rules.description: Protected routes and mutations must establish identity and authorization on the server. Client-provided user IDs, roles, tiers, or ownership claims must not be trusted.

rules.title: Validate external input
rules.description: API parameters, request bodies, webhook payloads, environment-derived values, and third-party responses must be validated before entering domain or persistence code.

rules.title: Keep persistence changes compatible and atomic
rules.description: Schema and migration changes must preserve existing data, define intentional nullability and defaults, and use transactions when a business operation requires multiple writes to succeed together.

rules.title: Protect billing and subscription invariants
rules.description: Entitlements must derive from trusted server-side subscription state. Billing mutations and webhooks must be authenticated, idempotent, and resilient to retries or out-of-order delivery.

rules.title: Avoid dependency and module cycles
rules.description: New dependencies must have a clear purpose, and module changes must not introduce circular imports, forbidden layer crossings, duplicate platform capabilities, or unnecessary client bundle weight.

rules.title: Keep secrets and sensitive data out of outputs
rules.description: Secrets, tokens, credentials, private customer data, and sensitive authentication details must not be committed, logged, returned by APIs, or exposed to browser bundles.

rules.title: Verify behavior at the correct layer
rules.description: Behavioral changes need focused tests. Tests should cover authorization failures, validation boundaries, domain invariants, and error paths in addition to the successful path.

rules.title: Make failures observable and safe
rules.description: External calls and background work need bounded failure handling, useful structured logs without sensitive data, and user-facing errors that do not leak internals.
